skill.name: waapi
skill.description: Web Animations API adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when authoring element.animate() motion, Animation currentTime seeking, document.getAnimations(), KeyframeEffect timing, fill modes, or native browser animations that must render deterministically in HyperFrames.
skill.summary: Web Animations API pattern guide for building seek-compatible animations in HyperFrames video compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 26 static analysis findings are false positives. The 19 backtick instances flagged as external_commands are code examples in markdown documentation, not runtime command execution. The 2 hardcoded URL findings are MDN reference links in a Credits section. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance findings are pattern-matching false positives on technical keywords in animation documentation. This skill is purely a documentation guide with no executable code or network behavior.

content.user_title: Write Web Animations with WAAPI for HyperFrames
content.value_statement: Creating deterministic browser animations for video rendering requires precise timing and seek support. This skill provides Web Animations API patterns that work with the HyperFrames adapter for frame-accurate animation playback.
actual_capabilities: Create seek-compatible WAAPI animations using element.animate() with finite iterations
Model animation timing with delay, duration, easing, and fill modes
Implement staggered animations across multiple elements with calculated delays
Generate frame-accurate animations from structured data sources
Use KeyframeEffect and Animation objects for precise playback control
limitations: Does not support infinite iterations or looping animations
Cannot use animation.finished promises for render-critical state changes
Does not work with requestAnimationFrame or performance.now() clocks
Layout-animating properties are less performant than transforms and opacity

use_cases.title: Motion Design for Video Content
use_cases.description: Designers creating animated video compositions with the Web Animations API.

use_cases.title: Data-Driven Animation Generation
use_cases.description: Developers generating animations programmatically from structured data sources.

use_cases.title: Lightweight DOM Animation
use_cases.description: Frontend engineers needing native browser animations without external libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fade-In Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WAAPI animation that fades in a div element over 2 seconds with a cubic-bezier easing and fill both mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a simple fade-in effect for a new user

prompt_templates.title: Staggered List Reveal
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a WAAPI stagger animation where each item in a list fades in and slides up with a delay of 80ms between items.
prompt_templates.scenario: Animating a list of items with staggered entry timing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyframe Path Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WAAPI animation with multiple keyframe offsets that moves an element through a sequence of positions while varying opacity and scale.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-step animation following a defined path

prompt_templates.title: Data-Driven Timeline Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate WAAPI animations from a JSON array of animation segments, each with start time, duration, easing, and keyframe definitions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Programmatic animation generation from structured data

output_examples.input: Create a fade-in animation for a welcome screen
output_examples.output: A WAAPI animation that transitions an element from transparent to fully opaque over 1.5 seconds using a smooth cubic-bezier easing curve.

output_examples.input: Animate a list of items one after another
output_examples.output: Multiple WAAPI animations where each list item starts 80ms after the previous one, creating a cascading reveal effect from top to bottom.

best_practices: Always use finite duration and iterations to ensure seek-compatible playback.
Set fill to both so seeked animation states persist across frames.
Pause animations after creation to let the adapter control timing.
anti_patterns: Using infinite iterations which cannot be seeked to a specific video frame.
Depending on animation.finished promises for render-critical DOM changes.
Running separate animation clocks with requestAnimationFrame or timers.

faq.question: What is the WAAPI runtime adapter for HyperFrames?
faq.answer: It is an adapter that seeks document-level Web Animations API animations by setting each animation currentTime property to match video frame time.

faq.question: Why must animations use finite iterations?
faq.answer: Infinite iterations cannot be seeked deterministically because the adapter needs a finite timeline to map each frame to a specific position.

faq.question: How do I control when an animation starts in a clip?
faq.answer: Use the delay property in your animation options to offset the start time within the clip timeline.

faq.question: Can I use CSS animations instead of WAAPI?
faq.answer: CSS animations work differently in HyperFrames. WAAPI provides more precise programmatic control through JavaScript keyframes.

faq.question: Does WAAPI support easing functions?
faq.answer: Yes. WAAPI supports cubic-bezier curves and standard CSS easing keywords like ease, ease-in, and ease-out.

faq.question: How do I debug WAAPI animations in HyperFrames?
faq.answer: Run npx hyperframes lint and npx hyperframes validate to check for common animation issues.
